issues.summary: Core BTR pipeline function exceeds 200 lines; consider decomposing into smaller, named stage functions
issues.suggestion: Extract each of the 6 BTR stages into standalone functions with explicit inputs/outputs
evidence: Main pipeline function is ~350 lines and mixes stage orchestration with error handling

issues.summary: File exceeds 2000 lines; extract domain-specific modules
issues.suggestion: Split into job-lifecycle.ts, retry-policies.ts, and metrics-reporter.ts
evidence: File contains job scheduling, retry logic, dead-letter handling, and metrics all in one module

issues.summary: Hook mixes transport concerns with UI state
issues.suggestion: Extract a transport-agnostic state machine; let the hook only bind state to React
evidence: Hook manages SSE connection, polling fallback, reconnection logic, and UI state in one unit

issues.summary: rectifyBirthTime is a long procedural sequence
issues.suggestion: Introduce explicit pipeline stages with early-exit helpers
evidence: Function is 78 lines of sequential logic without clear stage boundaries

issues.summary: Several internal functions use implicit any for ephemeris result shapes
issues.suggestion: Add explicit interfaces for ephemeris return types
evidence: Functions returning raw ephemeris data lack return-type annotations

issues.suggestion: Split into focused effects or a reducer
evidence: Single useEffect handles form init, validation, and navigation

issues.summary: Some branches return raw Error while others use AppError
issues.suggestion: Standardize to AppError hierarchy throughout middleware
evidence: Legacy throw new Error() remains in token validation path

issues.summary: Zod schemas and TypeScript interfaces are not co-located
issues.suggestion: Co-locate Zod schemas with their TypeScript interfaces
evidence: Type definitions and validation schemas are in separate files, risking drift

strengths: Clear module separation between web, api, and worker
judgment.dimension_character: Good overall structure with some overly large functions
judgment.score_rationale: Core pipeline and queue manager are too large; most other modules are well-abstracted

strengths: Clean separation of concerns between apps
judgment.dimension_character: Some complex files need better structure
judgment.score_rationale: Stream hook and orchestrator mix too many concerns

strengths: Good use of TypeScript strict mode
judgment.dimension_character: Some implicit any remain in complex calculations
judgment.score_rationale: Vedic astrology engine and some legacy areas lack full type coverage

strengths: Good variable naming in most files
judgment.dimension_character: Generally readable with occasional large effects
judgment.score_rationale: Most functions are small and focused; a few UI components need splitting

judgment.dimension_character: Mostly consistent with minor legacy exceptions
judgment.score_rationale: Auth middleware has a few legacy throw statements

strengths: Good use of Zod for validation
judgment.dimension_character: Types and schemas could be more tightly coupled
judgment.score_rationale: Zod schemas and TypeScript interfaces are in separate files

judgment.dimension_character: Solid mid-level design with room for improvement
judgment.score_rationale: Component structure is good; some hooks are too large
Web/API/Worker separation is clean; shared types are well-organized

judgment.dimension_character: Generally clear naming with minor inconsistencies
judgment.score_rationale: Most names communicate intent; a few generic names exist
Dependencies are up-to-date and well-chosen
Test coverage is insufficient for BTR engine and streaming logic
Endpoints follow consistent patterns
Auth is consistently applied across routes
A few files show signs of AI generation with suboptimal patterns
Few legacy patterns remain
Packages are logically organized
Initialization is straightforward
Some variation in patterns across modules
BTR logic is complex but well-explained
Minor outliers exist but conventions are mostly followed
